__fixture__.name: version_skew_outside_window_denied
__fixture__.scenario: A remote_agent_primary reconnect attempts to re-advertise a protocol range whose maximum has been dropped from the supported skew window since the prior session opened (the desktop client was upgraded mid-session). The mixed-version envelope cited on the reconnect falls outside the supported window in artifacts/compat/version_skew_register.yaml. Admission denies with remote_agent_mixed_version_envelope_outside_window. No new session id is issued; the reconnect is recorded so a support bundle or install-review surface can render a concrete repair affordance (upgrade the remote agent) rather than a generic 'not available' chip.

denial_reasons.repair_affordance_label: Upgrade the remote agent to a version within the supported protocol window (see artifacts/compat/version_skew_register.yaml); the desktop client moved past the prior window during the offline period.

conformance_tests.description: A version_skew_outside_window reconnect MUST include remote_agent_mixed_version_envelope_outside_window in denial_reasons.
A version_skew_outside_window reconnect MUST NOT issue a new_session_id; re-admission requires a fresh hello after the agent upgrades.
The repair affordance SHOULD point to the version-skew register so the reviewer can see which window applies.
Silent downgrade to the nearest compatible protocol is forbidden; denial is the correct behaviour.
